key_points: Area formulas are derived using geometric transformations
Parallelograms can be converted into rectangles with the same base and height
Two congruent triangles can form a parallelogram
Complex shapes can be decomposed into simpler geometric figures
Area relationships help solve problems involving equal areas
examples: Two triangles with the same base and height have equal area
Area of rhombus with diagonals 24 cm and 18 cm = 216 cm²
Area of trapezium with bases 20 cm and 14 cm and height 10 cm = 170 cm²
Area of composite polygon found by dividing into triangles
formulas: Trapezium → Area = 1/2 × height × (sum of parallel sides)
exam_tips: Understand derivation of formulas rather than memorizing them
Use geometric reasoning when solving area comparisons
Draw auxiliary lines to divide irregular shapes
Check that dimensions correspond to the correct formula
